human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for grocery promotion boundary discipline, source quality, circular/shelf/display/POS/coupon/loyalty/scale/order evidence handling, refund and promotion authority controls, recall and food-safety escalation, fraud-routing posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to grocery promotion execution, circular, shelf, display, POS, coupon, loyalty, scale, vendor-funding, and order evidence review, customer-impact exception handling, completion evidence, and refund, fraud, food-safety, recall, or policy escalation.

Authority sources cover FTC deceptive-pricing, bait-advertising, free-offer, and order guidance, NIST Handbook 130 current 2026 price verification and unit-pricing references, USDA FNS SNAP retailer training, FDA Food Code and recall references, FSIS recall references, and GS1 item-identity standards used in grocery promotion workflows.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete inputs, missing completion evidence, circular/coupon/POS mismatches, refund and coupon policy exceptions, recall and stop-sale handling, order and payment mismatches, high-volume ad and display sets, terminology fidelity, fraud-boundary escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/grocery-and-food-retail/grocery-promotions-specialist/ so grocery promotion execution remains separate from category pricing, promotion strategy, guest services, fraud, food safety, and operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around promotion strategy, price overrides, offer-stacking, refund and coupon exceptions, fraud adjudication, SNAP/EBT legal interpretation, food-safety disposition, and recalled-product handling.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated grocery promotions specialist that supports evidence-backed promotion execution, customer-impact exception handling, and safe escalation for refund, fraud, food-safety, recall, tender, and policy boundaries.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
